summary: A news article detailing a lawsuit against Stewart and Lynda Resnick over their profitable use of the Kern Water Bank during a California drought. Lynda Resnick dismisses the litigation as a nuisance driven by jealousy over their immense agricultural and marketing success, noting their strong legal defense.

principles.statement: Massive success inevitably inspires jealousy and nuisance attacks.
principles.rationale: When a business achieves dominant market share and wealth, competitors will often resort to frivolous lawsuits motivated by envy rather than merit.
principles.source_quote: That kind of success... can inspire jealousy, and likely motivated this most recent 'nuisance' lawsuit.

principles.statement: Maintain strong in-house counsel to defend your business.
principles.rationale: Because success attracts jealous attacks, having an excellent internal legal team is the key to consistently winning disputes.
principles.source_quote: There are very jealous people out there. But we usually win because we have such good in-house counsel.

signature_quotes.text: We’ve done more for the pistachio than anyone ever since it was planted in the Garden of Eden.
signature_quotes.context: Defending her company's massive impact on the pistachio market.

signature_quotes.text: My husband should be canonized for all the work he’s done.
signature_quotes.context: Praising Stewart Resnick's contributions to their agricultural empire.

signature_quotes.text: There are very jealous people out there. But we usually win because we have such good in-house counsel.
signature_quotes.context: Responding to a lawsuit filed by a competing pistachio processor.
